description_long: This is a large, four-engine military surveillance airplane with a high-mounted straight wing and a distinctive circular radar dome mounted above the fuselage. The aircraft is painted in a light gray color with red star insignias on the tail and wings, indicating its likely origin. The fuselage is robust and cylindrical, with a forward-mounted, multi-windowed cockpit. The tail features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The engines are mounted in pairs under each wing, and the landing gear appears to be retractable, though not fully visible. The overall design suggests a focus on airborne early warning and control operations.
description: A large, gray military surveillance airplane with a circular radar dome above the fuselage, four underwing engines, and red star insignias.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular radar dome above the fuselage, four underwing engines, and the forward cockpit windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the high-mounted straight wings, the radar dome, and the red star insignia on the tail and wing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and the full wingspan with engines visible.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the tail section, radar dome, and the overall length of the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two on each wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main and nose gear units implied.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
